heading: Rights of declarants; notifications; Government accountability
label: 4206 Rights of declarants; notifications; Government accountability

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: A person who has filed a declaration that meets the requirements of sections 4201 through 4204 of this title shall have the rights stated in this section.

children.heading: Notice of decision not to pursue
children.label: b Notice of decision not to pursue
children.text: If, after review, the Attorney General concludes that the information contained in a declaration should not be pursued in a civil or criminal proceeding, the Attorney General shall so notify the declarant in writing and shall provide a brief statement of the reasons that the declaration will not be pursued.

When the United States obtains a judgment, order, or settlement based in whole or in part on a valid declaration filed under , the Attorney General shall notify the declarant in writing of such fact.section 4201 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the Attorney General’s determination of the amount of the award due the declarant under subsection (c) or (d) of upon recovery by the United States; andsection 4205 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a short statement of reasons for the amount of the award.

children.heading: Notice of pendency of investigation or proceeding
children.label: d Notice of pendency of investigation or proceeding

children.text: there is a pending investigation or proceeding in the course of which the declarant’s allegations are being addressed; or
the declarant’s allegations have not yet been addressed.

In the case of a valid declaration filed not more than 3 years after , the Attorney General shall send notification to a declarant pursuant to subsection (d) not later than 3 years after the date of filing of the declaration.November 29, 1990

children.label: A
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), in the case of a declaration filed more than 3 years after , the Attorney General shall send notification not later than 1 year after the date of filing of the declaration.November 29, 1990

children.label: B
children.text: If the Attorney General certifies that it is in the interest of the United States to give further consideration to the information provided in the declaration for an additional 90-day period, the Attorney General shall so notify the declarant in writing.

children.heading: Confidentiality of notices
children.label: f Confidentiality of notices
children.text: All notices provided to a declarant under this section shall be kept confidential by the declarant in the same manner, and subject to the same penalties, as the declaration under .section 4203 of this title
